text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 21 September 2025 

Dr Nisha Patel 
Consultant Neurologist  
Riverside Neurology Clinic  
St. George’s Hospital, Blackshaw Road, London SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Nisha Patel, 

Mr Andrew Collins, DOB: 14-08-1996, Hospital No: K842117 NHS No. 9087764321  
Flat 12, 3 Willow Court, Kingston upon Thames, KT1 2AB  

Diagnosis: 

Focal epilepsy with secondary generalisation

Aetiology: 

Unclear aetiology; normal MRI brain (2024) and normal routine EEG; extended sleep-deprived EEG pending

Other Medical Problems: 

Mild asthma (inhaled budesonide/formoterol), GORD on omeprazole 20 mg OD

Management Plan: 

- Continue Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily. We discussed potential dose optimisation if further events occur; he prefers no change today pending EEG.  
- Safety: reinforced SUDEP and seizure first aid, showers instead of baths, avoid sleep deprivation and excess alcohol.  
- Driving: has not been driving; advised to continue to follow DVLA guidance and self-declare if applicable.  
- Arrange sleep-deprived EEG and drug level (for adherence context), FBC/U&E/LFT as baseline monitoring.  
- Review in clinic in 12 weeks, earlier if further seizures or adverse effects.  

History and Current Status: 

This 29-year-old right-handed gentleman with focal epilepsy reports overall improvement since commencing Levetiracetam. He remained seizure-free for three months after starting Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily, before experiencing a generalised tonic-clonic seizure three Tuesdays ago, preceded by a cluster of absences. He describes the absences as brief behavioural arrest with staring and loss of awareness lasting 10–20 seconds, occurring several times that afternoon, followed later by the convulsive event at home; postictal confusion lasted approximately 30 minutes without injury. There was no intercurrent illness, sleep loss, or alcohol excess. He has had two seizures in the last three months in total when accounting for the convulsive event and one earlier brief focal impaired-awareness episode two months prior. No myoclonic jerks reported. No status epilepticus. Medication adherence reported as good; no adverse effects beyond mild irritability in the first fortnight, now resolved. Family history negative for epilepsy. Birth and development unremarkable. 

Examination today is normal, including neurological assessment. Weight stable; BP 122/76. 

I will update you following neurophysiology and bloods. Please contact me if there are any concerns before his next appointment.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: He has had two seizures in the last three months in total
He remained seizure-free for three months after starting Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily, before experiencing a generalised tonic-clonic seizure three Tuesdays ago, preceded by a cluster of absences

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly quantifies recent seizures: "He remained seizure-free for three months ... before experiencing a generalised tonic-clonic seizure three Tuesdays ago ... He has had two seizures in the last three months in total when accounting for the convulsive event and one earlier brief focal impaired-awareness episode two months prior." Therefore, within the last 3 months, total seizures = 2. Convert to allowed format: 2 per 3 month. No higher-frequency alternative is given. Not seizure-free for 6 months, so not 'seizure free' label.
seizure_frequency_number: He has had two seizures in the last three months in total
reference: He remained seizure-free for three months after starting Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily, before experiencing a generalised tonic-clonic seizure three Tuesdays ago, preceded by a cluster of absences
